title: polyalthia fruticosa
intro: Polyalthia fruticosa, also known as the false ashoka, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains various bioactive compounds such as polyphenols and flavonoids, offering potential health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Traditional herbal preparations of Polyalthia fruticosa include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Polyalthia fruticosa are attributed to its ability to modulate the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a subject of interest in modern herbal medicine.
